question_text: Which statement best describes the particle model of matter?

options.label: A
options.text: All matter is made of tiny particles that are always stationary and tightly packed together

options.label: B
options.text: All matter is made of tiny particles that are always moving and have spaces between them

options.label: C
options.text: Only gases are made of particles; solids and liquids are continuous

options.label: D
options.text: Particles in solids move faster than particles in liquids

correct_answer: All matter is made of tiny particles that are always moving and have spaces between them
explanation: The particle model of matter explains that all substances (solids, liquids, and gases) are made of tiny particles. These particles are constantly moving in random directions and have spaces between them. The differences between solids, liquids, and gases come from how tightly packed the particles are and how fast they move, not from whether they have particles or not.
